title: Association of single nucleotide polymorphisms in ITLN1 gene with ischemic stroke risk in the Chinese population
author: Ye Tian
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: In this study, we designed PCR primers in the -3308bp region upstream of the transcription start site at the promoter region of  INLT1  gene. The target fragment was amplified by PCR system and identified by agarose gel electrophoresis. Sanger sequencing was then performed, and the obtained sequences were compared with the reference sequences from the NCBI website to determine the SNPs in  INLT1  gene promoter. Logistic regression analysis was used to assess the association between  ITLN1  polymorphisms and IS risk. By comparing with the Sanger sequencing results and the reference base sequence of the NCBI website, we found 14 SNPs satisfying HWE in  INLT1  gene promoter. Logistic regression analysis results showed that  ITLN1  (rs6427553, rs7411035 and rs4656958) were associated with a decreased risk of IS, especially in people less than 60 years old and in males. In a short, we found a reduced correlation between  ITLN1  variants (rs6427553, rs7411035 and rs4656958) and IS risk in the Chinese subjects, laying more foundations for  ITLN1  gene as a biomarker for ischemic stroke.
